title: RIOT Bitcoin Holdings and Balance Sheet Strength Across Earnings Cycles
text: One of RIOT's most distinctive earnings characteristics has been the company's substantial and growing Bitcoin holdings, which have increased dramatically across recent quarters as the company has transitioned toward infrastructure operations. At the end of 2024 (Q4), RIOT held 17,722 bitcoins valued at approximately $1.7 billion. By Q1 2025, holdings increased to 19,223 bitcoins. By Q1 2026, the company maintained 15,679 bitcoins despite treasury liquidation activities, valued at approximately $1.1 billion. As of December 31, 2025 (full-year 2025), the company held 18,005 bitcoins worth approximately $1.6 billion. The variation in holdings reflects both organic mining production and strategic capital allocation decisions, including treasury sales to fund operations and facility expansion. The Bitcoin holdings represent a significant source of shareholder value, providing implicit equity value independent of operational earnings. The 141% increase in Bitcoin holdings during 2024, despite a 27% decline in bitcoin production due to the halving event, demonstrates management's commitment to building a strategic Bitcoin position. Additionally, the company maintains substantial cash positions, with $282.5 million in cash reported at Q1 2026 and $309.8 million reported at full-year 2025. The combination of Bitcoin holdings and cash reserves provides financial flexibility for strategic investments and opportunistic data center lease signings.
